instName: ﻿St. Joseph's Ursuline Convent and Young Ladies' Academy
instNote: ﻿This house was established 22d August, 1857, by a colony from the well known institute of the Ursuline Nuns, near Fayetteville, Brown Co., Ohio. The course of instruction embraces all the branches usually taught in such institutes. Connected with the Academy is a Day and Free School.

instName: ﻿Ursuline Convent, Female Academy, Day and Boarding School
instNote: ﻿This institute was founded by a colony from the Ursuline Convent, at St. Louis, and the schools were opened since April last. Besides their Academy and Boarding School, in which the higher branches of a finished education are taught, the nuns conduct the parish schools of the congregation at Alton.
